title: Session 23: Linear Approximation
content:
« Previous | Next »
Overview Linear approximation is a powerful application of a simple idea. Very small sections of a smooth curve are nearly straight; up close, a curve is very similar to its tangent line. We calculate linear approximations (i.e. equations of tangent lines) near x=0 for some popular functions; we can then change variables to get approximations near x=a.

Clip 3: Linear Approximation and the Definition of the Derivative
